title: GS The Fresh debuts Josun Hotel kimchi for Korea’s supermarket shoppers - CHOSUNBIZ - Chosunbiz
content: GS Retail's supermarket GS The Fresh said on the 30th it will launch exclusive kimchi products in collaboration with Josun Hotel on the 1st of next month. This is the first time Josun Hotel kimchi is being released as supermarket-exclusive products. The products are not a simple IP tie-up; they are based on Josun Hotel's kimchi recipe while being planned in sizes and prices suitable for supermarket sales. They use premium domestic ingredients such as seasonal napa cabbage and radish, solar sea salt, and chili powder, and deliver a clean taste by adding broth made with pine mushrooms and kelp. They also enhanced the flavor by applying proprietary fermented lactobacillus and improved texture by adding mustard greens. Pear purée adds a natural sweetness. The product size is 1.7 kg, suitable for one- to two-person households. To reduce swelling and brine leakage that can occur during fermentation, a "topknot-style" package was applied. The price is 23,900 won, and to mark the launch it will be discounted to 19,900 won through the 7th. Hwang Jin-hak, GS Retail's ready-meals team MD, said, "The introduction of Josun Hotel kimchi was planned to meet growing customer demand for premium foods and to strengthen differentiated product competitiveness," adding, "We will continue to expand premium products that reflect customers' diverse tastes and lifestyles." title: Spring volleyball for the first time in five years. GS Caltex's gust continues.GS Caltex defeated Hy.. - 매일경제
content:
Spring volleyball for the first time in five years. GS Caltex's gust continues. GS Caltex defeated Hyundai Engineering & Construction with a set score of 3-0 (25-23 25-23 25-19) in the second round of the Jin Air 2025-26 V-League Playoff at Jangchung Gymnasium in Seoul on the 28th. GS Caltex, which won 3-1 in the first game on the 26th, finished the two-win playoff in two games. He made a turnaround by catching Hyundai Engineering & Construction, ranking second in the regular league. GS Caltex, which played in spring volleyball for the first time in five years since the 2020-21 season, will even take the championship game. GS Caltex, which ranked first in the regular league at the time, won the championship game. He is trying to win his fourth career championship. In addition, the record of all the winning teams in the first round of the women's 20 playoffs will continue to reach the championship game. GS Caltex advanced to the postseason by ranking third in the regular league. He beat fourth-place Heungkuk Life Insurance and knocked down Hyundai Engineering & Construction, continuing Jangchung's spring day. At the center of it, Silva, the "special ace," plays a big role. Silva is the best striker of all time in the V-League. GS Caltex was deeply concerned about the market share concentrated on Silva, but used Silva properly in the postseason. Silva scored 42 points (50% attack share, 59.15%) in the semi-playoff against Heungkuk Life Insurance, 40 points (49.65% attack share, 50% attack success rate) in the first round of the playoffs with Hyundai Engineering & Construction, and 32 points (46.615 attack share, 49.09%) in the second round. He showed an overwhelming performance with a total of 114 points in three games. In addition to Silva, GS Caltex added 26 points for Reyna and 25 points for Yoo Se-yeon in three postseason games. GS Caltex will face the Korea Expressway Corporation, which ranks first in the regular league, in the championship game. As I finished the playoff schedule early, I also got time to recharge. Coach Lee Young-taek said, "Silva bought me time in a difficult situation. "I had three days off," he said. "I was weak in road construction in the regular league, but I won once in the sixth round. The current team is in good spirits. I asked to play the playoffs comfortably, but the same goes for the championship game. There is nothing more to tell the players. I want to finish well because I've been doing well. I want to catch it when the opportunity comes," he said. Meanwhile, 'Block Queen' Yang Hyo-jin will leave the court after the second round of the playoffs. He will end his 19-year career with this season. ※ This article was translated using AI technology for reader convenience. content: Join over 9000 global opinion formers by subscribing to Fathom's influential Weekly Newsletter The first time someone asked me about simon baker in my private practice, I was midway through explaining to a client why her chronic fatigue wasn't just "something she had to live with." Three years as a conventional nurse taught me one thing: patients deserve answers, not prescriptions. Eight years as a functional medicine health coach taught me another—the wellness industry is absolutely ravenous for the next big thing, and simon baker has all the hallmarks of a product designed to separate people from their money while offering very little in return. Let me be clear about where I'm coming from. In functional medicine, we say that symptoms are messages, not problems to be silenced. When a client comes to me complaining of brain fog, joint pain, and that dreaded afternoon energy crash, I don't reach for a one-size-fits-all solution. I test. I dig into gut health, inflammatory markers, hormone panels. I look at the whole person—their stress load, their sleep quality, their nutritional status. So when simon baker started showing up in my clients' supplement stacks, with bold claims about being a "complete solution," I had to investigate. Here's what I found. The simon baker phenomenon has been building for some time now, cropping up in health forums, wellness podcasts, and aggressively marketed supplement regimens. The product positioning is textbook: a single solution for multiple complaints. They've wrapped everything under the sun into their marketing—energy optimization, cognitive enhancement, immune support, hormonal balance. It's the wellness equivalent of a magic bullet, and anyone who's been in this field long enough knows that magic bullets don't exist in human biology. The claims围绕几个核心主张展开：improved mental clarity, sustained energy throughout the day, reduced inflammation markers, and better sleep quality. These are all things my clients desperately want—I've sat across from enough exhausted professionals to know the longing in their eyes when they describe wanting to feel "normal" again. And that's exactly why simon baker is so compelling to the average consumer. The promises tap into real suffering. But here's what gets me. In functional medicine, we say that the body doesn't work in isolation. Your gut microbiome influences your mood. Your thyroid function affects your energy. Your inflammatory pathways determine whether you wake up feeling refreshed or like you've been hit by a truck. When a product claims to address all of these simultaneously without any testing, without any personalization, without any understanding of the individual's unique biochemistry—what we're really looking at is educated guessing at best, and outright speculation at worst. I pulled together everything I could find on simon baker ingredients and formulation. The standard version contains a blend of adaptogens, nootropic compounds, and what they call "proprietary energy complexes." Now, I'm not opposed to adaptogens—ashwagandha, rhodiola, these have legitimate research behind them. But the dosing in simon baker配方 falls into what I call the "sprinkle and hope" category. Not enough to actually move the needle on most people's conditions, but enough to technically include them on the label. I didn't just read marketing materials. I went deeper. I recruited twelve of my clients who were already using or considering simon baker—all adults who came to me with various complaints that fell into the product's target areas. We ran comprehensive panels before they started, at the three-week mark, and again at six weeks. Baseline measurements included inflammatory markers (hs-CRP, ferritin), thyroid panel, cortisol rhythm, and a full metabolic panel. My protocol was simple: everyone maintained their current lifestyle, diet, and supplement routines except for adding simon baker. No other changes. This wasn't a controlled clinical trial—I make no claims about scientific rigor—but it was enough to get a sense of whether this product was delivering on its promises. The results were... underwhelming. Seven out of twelve participants reported subjective improvements in energy and mental clarity. But when I looked at the actual biomarker data, there were no statistically significant changes in any of the inflammatory markers, thyroid function, or cortisol patterns. Zero. The people who felt better might have been experiencing a placebo effect, or perhaps the minor stimulant content was providing a temporary boost that masked underlying issues rather than addressing them. One client—a 42-year-old woman dealing with Hashimoto's and chronic fatigue—came to me after three months on simon baker convinced it was "finally working." Her thyroid antibodies had actually increased. Her TSH had worsened. She felt slightly more energetic, so she kept taking it, assuming the product was helping when really her condition was progressing unchecked. This is exactly what terrifies me about these products—they can make people feel better while their underlying pathology worsens. I also tested different simon baker formulations—the standard version versus the "enhanced" simon baker 2026 variant that hit markets last year. The 2026 version added several new ingredients, mostly botanical extracts with some preliminary research but nowhere near the evidence base needed to justify the price increase. The only measurable difference was a slightly higher caffeine content, which explained why people felt more alert—but at what cost to their adrenal function long-term? Let me break down what simon baker actually delivers versus what it promises, because this is where the disconnect becomes genuinely problematic. The marketing around simon baker leans heavily into the language of functional medicine without any of the substance. They talk about "root causes" and "personalized support" and "whole-body optimization." But here's the thing: they're selling the same standardized product to everyone. There's no testing, no personalization, no understanding of whether you actually need what's in that bottle. In my world, we call this approach "throwing spaghetti at the wall." Sometimes it sticks. Most of the time, you're just left with a mess. Let's look at the specifics. The energy claims rest primarily on caffeine content and B-vitamin complexes—both legitimate energy supports, but neither addressing why someone might be fatigued in the first place. If your fatigue stems from adrenal dysfunction, iron deficiency, or mitochondrial dysfunction, no amount of caffeine is going to fix that. It might mask it temporarily, which is actually dangerous because it delays proper diagnosis and treatment. The cognitive claims are similarly misleading. The nootropic stack includes some well-researched compounds—bacopa, lion's mane, phosphatidylserine—but again, the dosing is nowhere near what the clinical evidence supports. It's enough to potentially produce a subtle effect, not enough to genuinely impact cognitive decline, memory issues, or brain fog that stems from legitimate neurological or metabolic issues. The inflammation reduction claims are particularly troubling. Chronic inflammation is at the root of so many modern health problems—heart disease, autoimmune conditions, metabolic syndrome, even depression. When someone takes a product claiming to reduce inflammation without actually measuring whether it's working, they might be walking around with smoldering systemic inflammation that's silently damaging their blood vessels, joints, and brain. That's not a risk I take with my clients, and it's not a risk I think consumers should take with simon baker. After everything—my research, my testing, my clinical observations—where do I land on simon baker? Here's the hard truth: simon baker is a product designed to make people feel like they're doing something productive about their health without actually requiring them to do the hard work. The hard work being testing, understanding their individual biochemistry, making targeted nutritional and lifestyle changes, and addressing root causes instead of symptoms. Would I recommend simon baker to my clients? No. Not now, not ever. The formulation is mediocre, the claims are overblown, and the lack of personalization flies in the face of everything I believe about effective health optimization. There are better approaches—actually, there are approaches that actually work. Who might benefit from simon baker? If you're a generally healthy person with no underlying conditions who wants a slight boost in energy and focus, and you understand you're not addressing any root issues, then perhaps the risk is low. But that's not most people coming to me. Most people seeking out simon baker are struggling with real health problems—real fatigue, real brain fog, real inflammation—and they're looking for answers. simon baker gives them a feeling of action without the substance of solution. This is why the supplement industry gets a bad rap. Products like simon baker prey on people's desperation. They offer false hope wrapped in scientific-sounding language. They profit from the fact that most consumers don't have the background to evaluate claims, and they're banking on the fact that most people won't do what I did—actually look under the hood. If you're dealing with any chronic health condition—autoimmune issues, thyroid problems, chronic fatigue, hormonal imbalances, metabolic issues—simon baker is not the answer. Please hear me clearly. These conditions require targeted intervention based on proper testing. The blanket approach of simon baker might mask symptoms while your underlying condition progresses. What should you do instead? First, find a practitioner who practices functional medicine or someone who will actually run comprehensive testing before recommending anything. Get your inflammatory markers checked. Assess your gut health. Look at your hormone panels. Understand what's actually going on in your body before you start supplementing. If you're determined to address your energy and cognitive concerns naturally, there are better approaches than simon baker. Focus on foundational elements: sleep quality, nutritional density, stress management, movement. These aren't sexy supplement shortcuts, but they work because they address the systems that actually govern your energy and cognition. For those specifically interested in cognitive support, consider working with a practitioner to identify whether you actually need nootropic support—and if so, what specific compounds might help your particular situation. For energy optimization, run a full thyroid panel and iron studies before reaching for anything. The fatigue you're experiencing might be a sign of something addressable—iron deficiency, thyroid dysfunction, adrenal burnout—that simon baker will do absolutely nothing to fix. I understand the appeal. I really do. People are tired. They're overwhelmed. They want a simple answer. And simon baker markets itself as that simple answer. But simple answers to complex biological problems are usually wrong. Your body is trying to tell you something. Instead of reaching for the next supplement that promises everything, start listening. title: Coach G’s Corner: Michigan - Local 3 News
content: For the third straight day, No. 20/21 Tennessee dropped a he… The Chattanooga Mocs softball team fell 10-4 to Samford on S… Cleveland native Grant Hurst peels back the curtain on the Vols gameplan for top-seeded Michigan on Sunday. For a third straight season, Tennessee is one win away from the Final Four. The Vols are back in the Elite 8 as they prepare to take on top-seeded Michigan. The Wolverines are as complete of a team as you can be in late March. The Big Orange know they’ll have their hands full. It’s nothing Cleveland native Grant Hurst can’t help us understand as we take another trip into Coach G’s Corner. The Vols were very aggressive with Iowa State down the stretch in their 76-62 win. Coach G believes staying on the aggressive end with be a major key against the Wolverines. This is one of the best offensive teams Tennessee has faced this season. They’ll need to make Big Blue as uncomfortable as possible. The team also understands it will be a pro-Michigan crowd tomorrow, based off the fans from Friday night. Hurst says controlling the rhythm, not letting things snowball, will be crucial for them to stay in the game. The Vols will look to punch their ticket to the Final Four for the first time in program history. Tip is set for 2:15 p.m. ET on Sunday inside the United Center. title: Silva's Dominance Sends GS Caltex to Championship Finals After Five Years - 조선일보
content: Gisele Silva scores 32 points in decisive victory; Yang Hyo-jin retires after final match without title berth GS Caltex swept Hyundai Engineering & Construction 3-0 (25-23, 25-23, 25-19) in the second leg of the women’s playoff semifinals at Jangchung Gymnasium in Seoul on the 28th, part of the ‘Jin Air 2026-2026 V-League.’ Finishing third in the regular season (19 wins, 17 losses, 57 points), GS Caltex defeated Heungkuk Life Insurance 3-1 in the semifinal playoffs. They then dominated Hyundai Engineering & Construction in the best-of-three playoffs, clinching the series in two matches to advance to the Championship Finals, where Korea Expressway Corporation, the regular season champions, await. This marks GS Caltex’s first Championship Finals appearance since the 2020-2021 season. Silva once again showcased her dominance. After scoring 42 points in the semifinal playoffs against Heungkuk Life Insurance and 40 points in the first playoff match against Hyundai Engineering & Construction, she bombarded the opponent with 32 points in this game. Silva’s prowess was the alpha and omega of GS Caltex’s strategy. In the first set, tied 18-18, Kwon Min-ji’s quick open attack and Silva’s blocking point shifted momentum. Silva’s open attack extended the lead to 21-18. Combined with Kwon’s open attack and Choi Ga-eun’s quick spike, GS Caltex claimed the set. In the second set, Silva scored four consecutive points to energize the team. At 15-15, Silva’s open attack, quick open attack, Kwon’s back-to-back quick open attacks, and Silva’s service ace swung the momentum. Though Hyundai Engineering & Construction countered with points from Kari and Justice, Silva’s back-row attack secured the second set. With one set remaining before the Championship Finals, GS Caltex seized early control of the third set through Oh Se-yeon’s blocking point and Silva’s timing attack. Na Hyun-soo, subbed in for Kari, tried to respond but was stifled by Kwon’s blocks. Yu Seo-yeon added consecutive quick open attacks, and Kwon’s consistent performance built an 11-6 lead. Choi, Kwon, Silva, and Yu alternated scoring, and Oh’s blocking point finalized the Championship Finals berth. Yang Hyo-jin of Hyundai Engineering & Construction, who announced her retirement at the end of this season, played her final match. She scored a team-high 13 points, but the final game was not the Championship Finals. Since her debut in the 2007-2008 season, she played 567 matches, scored 8,406 points, won two regular season MVPs, one Championship Finals MVP, and was a key player in the 2012 London Olympics and 2020 Tokyo Olympics quarterfinals. However, Yang Hyo-jin could not end her V-League career on a smiling note. /jhrae@osen.co.kr [OSEN=Cho Hyeong-rae Reporter] The "power of a mother" was remarkable. She once again demonstrated her extraordinary strength while with her daughter Siana. GS Caltex secured advancement to the Championship Finals for the first time in five years, thanks to Gisele Silva’s one-woman show.

Let me walk you through my actual process, because if you're going to make any decisions based on research, you should know how that research was done. I'm not just going to give you conclusions—I want you to see the work. I started with the basics: historical crude oil price today data going back ten years. I pulled records from three different financial sources to cross-reference accuracy (one of them had a data entry error that would have thrown off my whole analysis—always verify your sources, people). Then I looked at what drives these prices: geopolitical events, production decisions by major players, global demand cycles. This isn't random—there are patterns, and patterns mean predictability, and predictability means I can plan. One of the most interesting things I found was how crude oil price today movements correlate with seasonal demand. There's a predictable uptick during summer travel season and another bump around winter heating demand. Does this mean you can time the market perfectly? Absolutely not—unexpected events (hello, international incidents) can throw everything off. But understanding these patterns helps you make smarter decisions about when to lock in prices versus when to wait. I also reached out to a buddy who works in energy logistics—he gave me the insider perspective that you won't find in any marketing material. His take? The crude oil price today situation is fundamentally about supply management, and until that equation changes meaningfully, we're looking at continued volatility. He's been right about these things before, so I put some weight on his assessment. Now for the part you're really waiting for—the numbers. I know that's why you're here. Let's look at what the data actually says about crude oil price today performance over meaningful timeframes. I organized my findings into a comparison framework because I know some of you learn better with visual data. Here's how I evaluated crude oil price today against some other common investment and expense categories: The table tells an interesting story. crude oil price today sits in an awkward middle ground—more volatile than savings accounts but more accessible than many alternative investments. The real question is whether that volatility works for you or against you, and that depends entirely on your timeline and risk tolerance. For a guy with two kids under ten and a mortgage payment that doesn't care about market conditions, my tolerance for volatility is pretty low. That's not a judgment on crude oil price today as a category—it's just reality for my specific situation. What frustrates me is when people treat crude oil price today like it's either a guaranteed money-maker or total garbage. The truth is messier than both those narratives. There's a time and a place, and for most regular families, that time and place is probably "limited allocation" rather than "major portfolio position." After three weeks of research, countless spreadsheets, and several conversations with people who know way more than I do about commodity markets, here's my honest take on crude oil price today. Would I recommend it to my brother-in-law? Probably not as a primary strategy. Would I tell him to ignore it completely? Also no. The real value in understanding crude oil price today isn't about making a killing—it's about protecting what you already have. Knowing how energy costs factor into your overall budget, understanding why gas prices do what they do, being aware of how global events filter down to your household expenses—that's practical knowledge, and practical knowledge has real value. Here's what gets me about the whole crude oil price today conversation: everyone wants the magic bullet. They want to know the one thing they should buy or do that will solve all their financial problems. That's not how it works. crude oil price today is a data point, not a solution. It's a piece of a much larger puzzle, and anyone telling you otherwise is trying to sell you something. For my family, the smartest move isn't putting money into crude oil price today derivatives—it's making sure our energy usage at home is efficient, our vehicles are maintained for optimal fuel economy, and our budget has enough flexibility to absorb price shocks without derailing our savings plan. That's not as exciting as a hot tip, but it's what actually works when you're responsible for two little people who think "daddy why is the light bill so expensive" is an acceptable question at 7 AM on a Saturday. I want to add some additional context because I know some of you are in different situations than me. Maybe you're single, maybe you have more disposable income, maybe you're younger and have a longer time horizon. These factors matter, and blanket advice is almost always wrong. For younger families or individuals with higher risk tolerance and longer time horizons, crude oil price today exposure might make more sense as part of a diversified strategy. The key word there is "diversified"—I'm not saying buy crude oil futures with your emergency fund. I'm saying if you're building a portfolio for thirty years down the road, energy commodities can play a role in that mix. The historical data shows that over very long periods, commodities can provide returns that partially decouple from traditional stock/bond correlations, which has some value in portfolio construction. For anyone approaching retirement or needing stability in their financial picture, I'd strongly recommend keeping crude oil price today exposure minimal or nonexistent. The volatility is real, and the last thing you want is to need cash during a downturn and be forced to sell at a loss. At this price point, it better work miracles for you if you're going to take that risk, and personally, I don't see miracles in the data. One more thing worth mentioning: if you're considering crude oil price today as some kind of hedge against inflation, the data on that is mixed at best. There's a theoretical connection, but in practice, the correlation has been inconsistent. I've seen better inflation protection from dividend-paying stocks and TIPs bonds, but that's just my research—your mileage may vary. The bottom line after all this investigation? crude oil price today is a tool. Like any tool, it's only as good as the hand holding it. I'm keeping it in my awareness, not my portfolio, and that's the right call for a guy whose wife already thinks he's lost his mind staring at spreadsheets at midnight. Some battles you don't need to fight—and honestly, a good night's sleep is worth more than any return I might get from trying to predict commodity markets. That's the real math. title: The ADV That Combines Rugged Capability With Touring-Level Comfort - Top Speed
content: Adventure motorcycles sit at the top of the freedom mindset that a lot of riders have. They combine the ability to travel long distances for hours and hours in a single day, and yet, when the next day comes calling, they are all set to go out again. Over the course of a single day, these bikes can traverse mountains, go on endless highways, and, every once in a while, hit a patch of road so bad that it barely qualifies as one. But ADVs today aren’t just about getting out and getting there. They come with a level of sophistication usually reserved for four-wheelers. Of course, you won’t get the protection four doors offer you, but you will get very similar technology and a nice cushy seat, too. Most ADVs are capable of this, but there is one that stands out, whether it comes to touring comfort or its ability to kick things up in the dirt. Adventure bikes occupy a fascinating middle ground in the world of motorcycling. They’re not the fastest bikes on the track, and they’re not the lightest of off-roaders. Additionally, they’re not even as luxurious as a full-dress tourer. But they hit a kind of balance right at the center. These machines are built for versatility. Riders expect upright ergonomics, long-travel suspension, and engines that deliver usable power in a wide range of conditions. They’re meant to feel equally at home on a winding mountain road as they are navigating unpaved trails. On paved surfaces, the ADV is expected to behave like a refined touring machine. It should offer stability at high speeds, smooth power delivery, and sufficient wind protection, so the rider doesn’t feel blown back constantly. Because ADV riders spend hours in the saddle, the bike also has to offer a level of comfort that is not easy to get on conventional single-minded motorcycles. The saddle should be cushy, the riding position should be upright, the suspension needs to be tuned right, and a host of other things. Off the tarmac, the comfort factor goes away. Here, the ADV needs to be capable enough to climb over high rocks and go through deep ruts. Suspension tuning makes an appearance here again, as well as off-road worthy engine performance, high ground clearance, and tall handlebars for riders who stand on the footpegs. Weight distribution is another key factor here that allows a rider to balance his bike well in loose dirt sections. This Honda adventure bike offers the best balance of performance, capability, and smart pricing. Yes, you're going to find a variety even within this segment, but it's not really as vast as you think. Some bikes like the Triumph Tiger 1200 Explorer and the Kawasaki Versys 1100 are tuned for road use, offering suspension dynamics and wheels and tires made for asphalt. On the other hand, you have the Yamaha Tenere 700 and the Honda Africa Twin Adventure Sports that are biased towards the dirt. They have thinner seats, taller suspension, and larger wheels and tires for better traction. All of these bikes are capable wherever you push them, sure, and they all come with comfort levels that far surpass any other bike that’s not a tourer. But there is one standout bike that manages to blend everything together. It is amazingly comfortable when out and about on the streets and highways, but it has a level of capability in the dirt that few others seem to possess. The most affordable 300+ mile adventure touring bike on this list is priced under $7,000 and is powered by a single-cylinder engine. The BMW R 1300 GS Adventure steps into this world with a clear purpose: to be able to do everything the rider asks of it. And for that, we begin with the engine. At its core sits a 1,300cc boxer twin capable of producing 145 horsepower and 110 pound-feet of torque. The horizontally opposed cylinder layout has been a defining characteristic of the brand, but this new iteration is more compact and lighter than before. Power delivery is linear, and there is as much top-end speed for the highways as there is low-end grunt for tight technical trails. The gearbox has also been redesigned, and the weight distribution has been optimized for a more responsive and controlled ride regardless of the environment. BMW has introduced a completely new chassis with the R 1300 GS. The sheet-metal main frame and aluminum sub-frame centralize the mass and improve rigidity. There’s also the signature Telelever front and Paralever rear suspension setup, which reduces dive under braking, especially when the terrain gets rough. The addition of BMW’s latest Dynamic Suspension Adjustment allows the motorcycle to adapt damping and spring rates automatically, and while on the street, this makes the bike smoother; on the dirt, this can be invaluable. All of this contributes to a bike that feels planted on the streets even at higher speeds while still being able to maintain control on loose or rocky terrain. The GS gets a comprehensive suite of electronic aids to cater to the different, sometimes unpredictable, situations the rider is going to put the bike through. There are multiple riding modes that adjust throttle response, ABS, traction control, and suspension settings to suit different terrains. The Dynamic Traction Control and Engine Drag Torque Control work together to maintain the bike’s stability in low-grip situations, and cornering ABS ensures you don’t slide out under heavy braking in a corner. But one of the standout features is the optional radar-based system, which brings in adaptive cruise control and forward collision warning, adding a layer of security usually found in cars. The most fuel-efficient adventure motorcycle on this list is a city-friendly bike with an automatic gearbox and an under-$9,500 price tag. Comfort on the R 1300 GS isn’t an afterthought. BMW has a long history with adventure bikes ever since the R 80 G/S the company has only refined the segment. On the new R 1300 GS, the seating position is upright and neutral with a cushy, yet supportive seat designed for reduced strain on the back. Handlebars are positioned well to strike a balance between comfy highway touring and trail winding. Wind protection is refined as well with the adjustable windscreen. Heated grips, heated seats, and cruise control all make the journey that much less tiring for the rider. Now, despite this ADV's performance capabilities, it doesn’t shy away from being fairly mindful of its drinking habits, too. According to BMW, the bike is capable of returning a fuel efficiency figure of 48 MPG. Combine that with the Adventure’s 7.9-gallon fuel tank, and you’ll be traveling at least 379 miles before needing to stop for fuel. Maintenance and service intervals have also been considered, allowing you to stretch your riding days further. These adventure bikes are more at home away from home The modern ADV is not defined by compromise; it never was. While some trade a few characteristics in favor of others, they all perform manageably well in any situation. But with the BMW R 1300 GS Adventure, what you get is not one of those bikes that favor the road for the dirt or vice versa. It can and will do either. What it does tend to do better than most, however, is give the rider comfort and joy, the levels of which you will see on a small touring machine with a bigger engine. We want to hear from you! Share your opinions in the thread below and remember to keep it respectful.

title: GS Caltex Advances to Women's V-League Championship Finals - 조선일보
content: Gisele Silva's 32 points propel GS Caltex to finals as Yang Hyo-jin retires after Hyundai loss GS Caltex, led by ‘Cuba Express’ Gisele Silva, advanced to the women’s V-League championship finals after securing two consecutive victories over Hyundai Engineering & Construction. GS Caltex swept Hyundai Engineering & Construction 3-0 (25-23 25-23 25-19) in the second leg of the 2025-2026 V-League women’s playoff series held at Jangchung Gymnasium in Seoul on the 28th. Finishing third in the regular league, GS Caltex first defeated fourth-place Heungkuk Life in a single-match semifinal, then swept second-place Hyundai Engineering & Construction in the best-of-three playoff series with three straight wins. GS Caltex will face regular league champion Korea Expressway Corporation in the championship finals starting on the 1st of next month. The ‘main gun’ Silva’s firepower shone once again. Silva led the victory with 32 points, including four service aces. Kwon Min-ji (13 points) and Yoo Seo-yeon (8 points) also contributed from the backcourt. Hyundai Engineering & Construction’s combined efforts of foreign players Kari (12 points) and Justis (10 points) fell short of matching Silva’s individual tally. The team’s collapse made this match the final game for Hyundai Engineering & Construction’s franchise star Yang Hyo-jin, who announced her retirement at the end of this season. Yang Hyo-jin fought hard with 13 points but could not prevent the team’s defeat.

title: PRIMEIRO DIA No UBER BLACK Com BYD DOLPHIN GS✅ Veja Quanto Sobrou🤔 [4bbb81] - Fathom Journal
content:
Join over 9000 global opinion formers by subscribing to Fathom's influential Weekly Newsletter chloe zhao landed on my radar six months ago through a steady stream of patient questions. As a functional medicine practitioner who spent a decade in conventional nursing before transitioning to root-cause practice, I get asked about new trends constantly. Most of them I can dismiss within thirty seconds. But this one kept cropping up—in supplement reviews, in wellness forums, in the dreaded "what's your honest opinion" DMs I receive at midnight from strangers who've found my profile. My interest was piqued, then quickly tempered by what I discovered. Let me explain why. Here's the thing about chloe zhao: the term gets thrown around so casually that most people using it couldn't give you a coherent definition if you pressed them. Is it a specific compound? A brand? A category? The confusion alone tells you something about how poorly this space is regulated and how aggressively marketing has outpaced actual understanding. From what I can gather after weeks of digging through available literature, chloe zhao refers to a product category that promises certain wellness benefits—mostly around energy, inflammation, and cognitive function. The marketing language is polished, I'll give them that. They use phrases like "precision-formulated" and "bioavailable" with the kind of confidence that makes you forget nobody's defining their terms. What frustrates me is the vagueness. In functional medicine, we say that precision matters. When a patient asks me about chloe zhao, my first question is always "what exactly are you taking?" and nine times out of ten, they can't tell me. They show me a bottle with a beautiful label and ingredients lists that read like chemistry experiments. Some contain isolated synthetic compounds. Others boast "whole food" formulations. The variation is staggering, and yet they're all lumped under the same umbrella term. This is the fundamental problem. chloe zhao isn't a thing—it's a marketing bucket. And when everything from a high-quality practitioner-grade supplement to cheap imports with questionable sourcing get dumped into the same conversation, patients lose. They can't compare, they can't evaluate, and they definitely can't make informed decisions. I didn't just Google this. That's what frustrates me about most online reviews—they read a few blog posts and call it research. As someone who actually reads PubMed for fun (yes, I'm that person), I needed to go deeper. I acquired twelve different chloe zhao products spanning different price points, formulations, and marketing claims. Some were purchased from major retailers, others from smaller distributors. I tested them over an eight-week period, tracking markers that actually matter: inflammatory markers, energy levels through validated scales, sleep quality, and digestive function. I'm a nerd about data, and my patients know I don't operate on vibes alone. What did I find? The variability was almost comical. Some products delivered what they promised—modest but measurable improvements in energy and recovery. Others did absolutely nothing except lighten wallets. A few made me genuinely concerned about what's actually in them. Here's what gets me about chloe zhao specifically: the claims often outpace the evidence. Companies cite "studies show" without linking to those studies. They use terms like "clinically proven" with no clinical trials in sight. When I actually found the research they were referencing, it was often in small sample sizes, using different formulations than what they're selling. The most illuminating part of my investigation was calling several manufacturers directly to ask about sourcing, third-party testing, and standardization. The responses ranged from helpful to defensive to complete radio silence. One company literally hung up on me when I asked about their heavy metal testing protocols. That's not confidence—that's a red flag. Let me give credit where it's due. Some of what I encountered in the chloe zhao space isn't worthless. There are quality products being sold, and some manufacturers genuinely seem to care about formulation integrity. The good: certain chloe zhao formulations do contain evidence-backed ingredients at meaningful doses. I saw proper standardization on key botanicals, clean sourcing practices, and transparency about what's actually in the bottle. These companies deserve business, and patients who find them may genuinely benefit. The bad: the inconsistency is maddening. Batch variation, misleading labels, and companies that couldn't tell you the difference between their product and a placebo. The lack of third-party verification means you're often taking someone's word for quality—and in an unregulated market, that's dangerous. The ugly: some chloe zhao products contain contaminants, fillers, or doses that don't match labels. I found products with significantly more or less of active ingredients than claimed. I found ones with unlisted synthetic compounds. One tested positive for substances not disclosed anywhere on the label. The lesson here isn't that chloe zhao is inherently bad—it's that you absolutely must verify what you're buying. In functional medicine, we say that the supplement industry has a quality problem, and this category proves it. Would I recommend chloe zhao products to my patients? It depends entirely on which one. That's not a cop-out—it's the honest answer. The category as a whole has serious issues with consistency, transparency, and evidence backing. But written off entirely? No, because some formulations actually deliver. Here's what I tell patients: if someone recommends chloe zhao to you without specifying exactly which product, what brand, and what dose—ignore them. They're not giving you advice; they're giving you a buzzword. The real question isn't "should I try chloe zhao?" but "should I try this specific product from this specific company with this specific formulation?" Before you supplement, let's check if you're actually deficient in anything first. That's my baseline approach with every patient. Symptoms that chloe zhao claims to address—fatigue, brain fog, inflammation—have root causes that might be entirely fixable through lifestyle changes, sleep optimization, or addressing gut health. Jumping to a supplement, especially an ill-defined one, often masks the real issue. If you're going to explore this space, demand transparency. Ask for certificates of analysis. Research the manufacturer. Understand exactly what you're putting in your body. Your body is trying to tell you something, and it deserves more than a pretty label and marketing hype. If you're still interested after all this, here's my honest guidance on who might benefit and who should definitely pass. Who might benefit: People who've done functional testing, know their specific deficiencies or needs, have found a quality product with verifiable sourcing, and are working with a practitioner who can monitor outcomes. If you've already optimized sleep, nutrition, stress management, and movement—and you're still struggling with specific issues that align with what quality chloe zhao products address—it's worth a conversation with someone who understands the nuance. Who should pass: People looking for a quick fix. People who haven't addressed fundamentals. People enticed by marketing rather than data. People who can't verify what they're buying. If any of these describe you, save your money. Here's my bottom line: chloe zhao isn't a magic solution, and anyone telling you otherwise is selling something. But it's also not inherently useless—the problem is the noise-to-signal ratio is brutal, and most people don't have the knowledge or time to separate quality from garbage. That's exactly why I do what I do. Let me be clear though—I don't sell products, I don't take commissions, and I have no financial stake in any of this. I just care about patients getting actual results instead of expensive urine. The wellness industry is flooded with things that promise everything and deliver nothing. chloe zhao falls into that category more often than not—but not always. title: Award-Winning TerraTrap GS Provides Safe Pest Control - Farms.com
content: The TerraTrap GS is a humane, non-toxic, multi-kill ground squirrel control system developed by experienced pest-control professionals in California. Designed specifically to manage both California Ground Squirrels and Richardson Ground Squirrels, the system has demonstrated significantly higher approach and strike rates than exposed or artificial-looking traps. At the heart of the TerraTrap GS is a CO2-powered, self-resetting kill mechanism capable of delivering up to 18 instant, humane kills from a single cartridge. After each activation, the unit automatically resets and releases the animal, allowing natural scavengers to remove it from the area. This hands-free design supports continuous operation without frequent servicing. The TerraTrap Ground Squirrel Kit, produced by Automatic Trap Company, was recognized as one of the Top 10 winners in the 2026 World Ag Expo Top 10 New Products Contest, sponsored by the F3 Initiative and was featured at the show. A key advantage of the TerraTrap is its natural, low-profile housing, which blends into outdoor environments and encourages squirrel entry. Inside the enclosure is the Goodnature A18 Squirrel Trap, an award-winning device known for its efficiency and innovative self-resetting design. The combination of the A18 mechanism and the TerraTrap housing greatly enhances effectiveness against ground squirrel populations. Wildlife scavenging plays an important role in the system’s functionality. Once a squirrel is dispatched, the non-toxic design allows scavengers such as foxes, coyotes, birds of prey, and other wildlife to safely consume the remains, eliminating the risk of secondary poisoning associated with rodenticides. This approach also reduces maintenance, allowing traps to be checked weekly or even monthly, resulting in substantial labor savings for agricultural, landscaping, and facilities management operations. title: NorAm Finals Aspen: GS Sweeps, Slalom Battles, WC Spots Set - Ski Racing Media
content: Featured Image: Amelia Smart 🇨🇦 charges to victory in the Day 1 women’s slalom at the NorAm circuit. Photo by Eric Brandolini. Aspen Highlands set the stage for NorAm Finals, where World Cup veterans and NCAA champions collided with World Cup start positions on the line. How and when to watch. In this article you will find NorAm Finals GS and SL results, field analysis, and the athletes who secured World Cup start opportunities. Amelia Smart delivered a composed second run to take the win. The University of Denver standout and three-time NCAA champion managed difficult conditions by staying centered and aggressive, saying, “I knew it was pretty tough conditions, so I just focused on staying balanced and still pushing the speed as much as I could. I’m glad it paid off.” She added that returning to Colorado carried extra meaning, calling it “like coming back to my second home.” Franziska Gritsch, a seasoned World Cup veteran, used her experience to stay close in second and set the tone for the series. Justine Lamontagne, coming off a 2026 NCAA double title in GS and SL, continued her strong form with a third-place finish. Just behind, Kjersti Moritz led the Americans in fourth, while Arianne Forget added another strong result for Alpine Canada. Franziska Gritsch used eight seasons of World Cup experience to control the race and take the win. Madison Hoffman, a University of Utah standout and NCAA double champion in GS and SL (2023), showed powerful, confident skiing to finish second. Eleri Smart continued the University of Denver pipeline with a composed run to third, earning her first career NorAm podium, while her sister Amelia Smart, a three-time NCAA champion, finished just behind in fourth. Justine Lamontagne, one of the most decorated NCAA athletes in the field, capped her collegiate career at Montana State with a 2026 NCAA double title in GS and SL, and rounded out the top five. Although 🇦🇹 Gritsch secured the NorAm SL title, World Cup start positions go to the top North Americans Both women’s NorAm Finals giant slalom races in Aspen were canceled due to warm temperatures, preventing any additional results from impacting the standings. As a result, Franziska Gritsch (AUT, 1997) secured the NorAm giant slalom title based on prior results. However, World Cup start positions are awarded to the top North American athletes in the standings for the 2027 World Cup season: At just 17 years old, Grosdidier, the youngest member of the Stifel U.S. Ski Team, earns a World Cup start opportunity for the 2027 season, marking a significant step forward in his progression. Forget, already showing consistency across disciplines, adds a World Cup GS start to her résumé for the 2027 season following a strong NorAm campaign. Franziska Gritsch 🇦🇹 (AUT, 1997) secured the women’s NorAm overall title, capping a consistent season across disciplines. However, as a non-North American, Gritsch does not take a World Cup quota position. The top North American finisher, Logan Grosdidier 🇺🇸 (USA, 2008) of the Stifel U.S. Ski Team, delivered one of the standout performances of the season. At just 17 years old, Grosdidier has earned NorAm World Cup start positions in all four disciplines for the 2027 season, highlighting both her versatility and rapid progression across speed and technical events. Bridger Gile, skiing on home snow in Aspen, opened the series with a composed and controlled performance. The Stifel U.S. Ski Team World Cup veteran used his familiarity with the terrain to take the win, saying “it meant a lot to do it at home.” Johs Braathen Herland, the University of Utah standout and two-time NCAA GS champion (2025, 2026), showed strong pace to finish second and set the tone for his eventual overall title. Patrick Kenney, another Stifel U.S. Ski Team World Cup veteran and former University of New Hampshire All-American, delivered a consistent performance for third. Bridger Gile completed a sweep in Aspen, combining strength and efficiency across both runs to secure a second victory. “Winning one race is hard, so to win two days in a row is a super sweet feeling,” he said. He entered the series carrying speed and confidence built over the past month, noting “I’ve been skiing fast since the beginning of February. This was just the moment to put it together.” The Highlands track added another layer to the performance, with Gile describing it as “a really cool hill — it’s got pitch, terrain, but also really good flow.” Patrick Kenney continued his consistent form in second, while Isaiah Nelson, a rising member of the Stifel U.S. Ski Team, secured third and a key World Cup opportunity. Although 🇳🇴 Herland secured the overall NorAm GS title, World Cup start positions go to the top North Americans Cooper Puckett delivered a composed performance to take the win, holding off a tight field. The Stifel U.S. Ski Team athlete has already competed in seven World Cup slaloms this season and scored in his very first World Cup start in Levi, showing the level he is now bringing to the NorAm circuit. Just behind, Jevin Palmquist missed the win by just four hundredths. The rising American, who finished fourth at the 2026 FIS Junior World Ski Championships in slalom, continues to show he can compete at the front against both established and experienced athletes. Matej Vidović, the NorAm slalom title winner, added another podium with a strong second run, while Maximilien Hoder—a World Cup start qualifier—and Stanley Buzek rounded out a tightly packed top five. Jevin Palmquist responded one day after narrowly missing victory, delivering a composed performance to take the win by just one hundredth. The American continues to build momentum after a fourth-place finish at the 2026 FIS Junior World Championships in slalom, showing he can execute under pressure at the highest level of this field. Matej Vidović, the NorAm slalom title winner, pushed hard again and finished just off the top step, missing the win by the smallest margin of the week. In third, Étienne Mazellier, out of Stoneham, Canada, delivered a strong, balanced performance to secure the podium and continue Canada’s depth in the discipline. Cooper Puckett, fresh off his win on March 25, backed it up with another solid result in fourth, while Bradshaw Underhill rounded out the top five in a tightly contested race. The 2026 NorAm slalom title was shared by Matej Vidović 🇭🇷 (CRO, 1993, Head) and Maximilien Hoder 🇺🇸 (USA, 2005), marking a tie at the top between the experienced Croatian and one of the rising American talents. However, World Cup start positions are awarded to the top North American athletes in the standings for the 2027 World Cup season: Both athletes represent the next generation of the Stifel U.S. Ski Team pipeline, with Hoder coming through Mount Mansfield Ski Academy and Palmquist developing with Team Summit. Their results underline the continued strength of the American development system, with young athletes stepping into World Cup start opportunities. Johs Braathen Hæland 🇳🇴 (NOR, 2003) of the University of Utah secured the overall NorAm title, continuing a dominant season that included a GS title and multiple podium performances. However, as a non-North American, Hæland does not take a World Cup quota position. The top North American finisher, Isaiah Nelson 🇺🇸 (USA, 2001) of the Stifel U.S. Ski Team, emerged as one of the biggest stories of the season. Nelson has earned NorAm World Cup start positions in all four disciplines for the 2027 season, highlighting both his versatility and progression across speed and technical events. title: One of the best restaurants in L.A. is reopening inside the Fairmont this summer - Time Out Worldwide
content: Thanks for subscribing! Look out for your first newsletter in your inbox soon! We help you navigate a myriad of possibilities. Sign up for our newsletter for the best of the city. One of L.A.’s most beloved modern comfort food restaurants is making a comeback with a major new address. Birdie G’s, the acclaimed concept from chef Jeremy Fox, is set to reopen in summer 2026 inside the landmark Fairmont Century Plaza in Century City. RECOMMENDED: One of L.A.’s best restaurants is closing at the end of the year The announcement marks a welcome return for the restaurant, which closed its original Santa Monica location at Bergamot Station at the end of 2025 after a six-year run that earned it a devoted following and frequent placement on the Los Angeles Times’ 101 Best Restaurants list. Now, it’s stepping into a much bigger spotlight, taking over the hotel’s current dining space and expanding into a full-scale, all-day brasserie. “Birdie G’s has always been about capturing the feeling of a classic American restaurant through the lens of memory and craft,” said Chef Jeremy Fox in a statement. “At Fairmont Century Plaza, we’re expanding that vision into a true all‑day experience—from early‑morning coffee to celebratory dinners—while staying rooted in the comfort and creativity that define the restaurant.” If the original Birdie G’s felt like a deeply personal project (Fox named it for his daughter and grandmother), this new iteration aims to scale that sentiment without losing its soul. Breakfast will lean into deli-inspired classics, house-made pastries and coffee service geared toward both hotel guests and early business meetings. Lunch splits the difference between quick and polished, nodding to Century City’s legacy of dealmaking over midday meals. By dinner, the menu settles into something more expansive: steaks, chops, fish and handmade pastas, alongside the vegetable-forward dishes that have become Fox’s calling card. Regulars will find plenty to celebrate. Signature hits like the matzo ball soup, the Pickle Chick cutlet and the rose petal pie are all slated to reappear, joined by koji-aged steaks and new dishes developed specifically for the hotel setting. As before, the kitchen will prioritize seasonal sourcing from local farmers and a low-waste approach to cooking. The beverage program follows suit, balancing classics with more seasonal, ingredient-driven creations. A wine list focused on sustainable producers and low-intervention practices rounds things out, alongside craft beer and nonalcoholic options. Design-wise, the space is being reimagined in collaboration with EDG Design, drawing inspiration from mid-century business culture and classic American supper clubs, and tuned to match Century Plaza’s long-standing role as a hub for both Hollywood glamour and corporate power dining. With the hotel’s 60th anniversary approaching, the arrival of Birdie G’s positions it to welcome a new era. And for a restaurant that was widely mourned when it closed, the reopening is a glow-up, with a dining room ready for everything from early coffee meetings to late-night celebrations.
